title: ROK government: trilateral data-sharing for DPRK missiles continues operations
summary: JKGA ROK Gov reports that South Korea's Ministry of National Defense reaffirmed routine operations of the US-Japan-ROK trilateral real-time missile-warning data-sharing system, with no DPRK launches affecting Korean Peninsula or Japanese EEZ during the reporting period.
contents:
# ROK government: trilateral data-sharing for DPRK missiles continues operations

## Operational Status
JKGA ROK Gov reports that the South Korean Ministry of National Defense (MND) confirmed routine, uninterrupted operation of the trilateral US-Japan-ROK real-time missile-warning data-sharing system. No DPRK missile launches occurred during the past 24 hours that affected the Korean Peninsula, the Sea of Japan or Japanese EEZ. Routine data-sharing exercises continued on schedule.

## Policy Posture
ROK MND statements emphasised continued readiness across all peninsula scenarios alongside ongoing sanctions enforcement against DPRK proliferation networks. The Unification Ministry reaffirmed willingness for dialogue with North Korea conditional on denuclearisation steps. Humanitarian assistance channels remain open consistent with prior policy.

## Strategic Context
The ROK government indicates that the ongoing US-Iran confrontation has not diluted attention to Korean Peninsula readiness. ROK refiners draw down strategic petroleum stocks and accelerate Saudi and UAE crude lifting in response to Brent crude's $126 spike. ROK leadership continues to engage Washington on secondary-sanctions implications for downstream linkages.
